tolerance.notes: Cartesian base geometry is pure linear arithmetic (base extents, dx, dy, cell_center/x_edge/y_edge/corner evaluate to single add+multiply chains), so 1e-14 is well within reach across all four libm/numeric stacks. A non-trivial drift here indicates an algorithm bug, not float-library jitter.

scope: Cartesian base only. The arakawa accessor runtime is parameterised over an ArakawaBaseGrid; all four bindings implement CartesianBase today. lat_lon-base composition has not landed in any binding (see discretizations/grids/arakawa/README.md — lat-lon fixtures are declaration-only). The harness exercises cell_centers, u_face, v_face, corners, the generic coord(loc, ...) accessor, neighbors(loc, ...), metric_eval, and per-stagger variable_location/variable_shape for all five staggers A/B/C/D/E.
